MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2022 Vacated

Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Andre Rucker; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Wabel Moussly (Motions)
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Wabel Moussly, Esq. (310.481.7600; wmoussly@wshblaw.com) of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendants.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Discussed. The court rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS HARM REDUCTION THERAPY CENTER, JEANNIE LITTLE, ANNA BERG, JIA BROUSSARD and PATT DENNING's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Andre Rucker:

Moving parties, defendants HARM REDUCTION THERAPY CENTER, JEANNIE LITTLE, ANNA BERG, JIA BROUSSARD, and PATT DENNING (collectively "Defendants"), seek a terminating sanction, or alternative sanctions, against plaintiff ANDRE RUCKER, pro se ("Plaintiff"), for failing to comply with this court's prior orders compelling responses to discovery and for the payment of sanctions. Plaintiff has not submitted an opposition to the motion. Upon review of this court's docket, the moving papers and the evidence submitted in support of the motion, the court makes the following findings and ruling.

Plaintiff filed his Complaint on December 2, 2020, which appears to assert a cause of action for negligent "patient dumping" against the defendants and seeks monetary damages and injunctive relief for the reinstatement therapy. Defendants jointly propounded Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents to Plaintiff, which collectively sought to obtain all facts, witnesses and documents supporting the claims being made against them. Plaintiff failed to respond to this discovery. On November 7, 2021, the court granted all motions to compel and for sanctions. Plaintiff was ordered to provide responses without objections and to pay monetary sanctions within ten (10) days. Plaintiff failed to timely comply with the court's orders. Counsel for Defendants sent a letter to Plaintiff advising him of his non-compliance with the order and requested that Plaintiff comply by a date certain. Plaintiff did not comply and Defendants filed the instant motion.

The court finds that Plaintiff's failure to respond to the discovery and with this court's orders compelling same is a misuse of the discovery process under subdivisions (d) and (g) of Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.010. Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030 sets forth the various options for sanctions.

Accordingly, Defendants' motion is GRANTED. IT IS ORDERED that pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030 (d)(3) Plaintiff ANDRE RUCKER's action is dismissed (with prejudice) in its entirety.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to sspaulding@bcsslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Motion For Ordering Anna Berg To Pay Me $100,000 For The Emotional Harm, Psychological Trauma And Punitive Damages She Caused Me Acutal Injury Although It Cannot Be Seen Physically Be Paid In Full May 5th 0221
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Isabella Urrea, Esq. (818-551-6038; iurrea@wshblaw.com) of Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman LLP for Defendants.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDRE RUCKER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; Motion For Ordering Anna Berg To Pay Me $100,000 For The Emotional Harm, Psychological Trauma And Punitive Damages She Caused Me Acutal Injury Although It Cannot Be Seen Physically Be Paid In Full:

Plaintiff's "motion for ordering Anna Berg to pay me $100000 For the emotional harm, psychological trauma and punitive damages she caused me actual injury although it cannot be seen physically Be paid in full" is denied. Plaintiff cites no statutory or legal basis for the requested relief and the motion fails to comply with California Rules of Court 3.1112 and 3.1350. In addition, Plaintiff fails to meet his burden of production to make a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any genuine issue of material fact. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

PLAINTIFF ANDRE RUCKER's MOTION Ordering Reinstatement Of Medical Treatment With Jia Broussard; Motion For Ordering Reinstatement Of My Therapy Treatments With Jia Broussard Motion For Duration Of These Proceedings If The Progress Past Wednesday Summary Judgment, That She Remain My Therapist Until I Am Healed To Be Adjudicated:

Plaintiff's "motion ORDERING Reinstatement of medical treatment with Jia Broussard" is denied. Plaintiff cites no statutory or legal basis for the requested relief and the motion fails to comply with California Rules of Court 3.1112. Further, the motion appears to be an untimely and improper motion for reconsideration of the Court's May 26, 2021 order, which denied Plaintiff's substantially similar motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
